etymology.description: 品(品) (pǐn, "three mouths") + 木(木) (mù, "tree") = chirping of birds in a tree -> original form of 噪(噪) (zào, "noise")
etymology.interpretation: The character 喿(喿) (zào, "used phonetically") combines 品(品) (pǐn, "three mouths") and 木(木) (mù, "tree") to represent the sound of birds chirping in a tree. The combination of multiple mouths and a tree suggests a setting where numerous birds are making noise among branches, aptly depicting the concept of chirping.
